sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented in available sources

special_mechanics.name: Star Uppercut System
special_mechanics.description: Little Mac can earn up to 3 stars by counter-punching opponents at specific times. Stars enable a powerful uppercut attack. Stars are lost when Mac is hit or knocked down.

special_mechanics.name: Heart/Energy Depletion
special_mechanics.description: Little Mac has a heart counter that decreases when hit, blocking punches, or throwing blocked/dodged punches. When depleted, Mac becomes exhausted (visual palette change) and cannot attack but can still dodge, duck, and block. Hearts are regained by avoiding opponent punches.

special_mechanics.name: Knockdown System
special_mechanics.description: Fighters are knocked down and must rise within 10 seconds. Third knockdown in a round = TKO. Mac can be knocked down maximum 4 times total per match; 4th knockdown = automatic loss.

special_mechanics.name: Defense Mechanics
special_mechanics.description: Little Mac can dodge left/right, duck, and block. Ducking is 1 frame faster than dodging, making it optimal in tool-assisted play but risky in real-time.

special_mechanics.name: Clock Stop Glitch
special_mechanics.description: Advanced TAS technique: rapid punches temporarily stop the match clock. A delayed punch after rapid punches can permanently stop the clock until specific events occur (knockdown, stun, damage, star uppercut).

special_mechanics.name: Gutter Technique
special_mechanics.description: TAS/advanced technique: holding Up raises opponent's guard, then releasing and performing a body blow lands against slow-reacting opponents. Up to 3 gutters possible before opponent reacts.

special_mechanics.name: Misdirection Technique
special_mechanics.description: Advanced TAS frame-perfect technique exploiting 2-frame button input windows. Alternating button combinations allow faster gutter execution against normally impossible opponents like Soda Popinski.

tier_notes: Tier 1 empirical/qualitative data. This is a non-platformer sports game (boxing) with no documented jump physics, gravity, or sub-pixel systems. All documented parameters relate to combat mechanics, not character movement physics. Game uses pattern recognition and timing-based combat rather than physics-driven movement. Developer: Nintendo R&D3 (also listed as Nintendo IRD). Little Mac is 17 years old, weighs ~107 lbs. NES hardware limitations prevented recreation of arcade graphics; character made smaller and named 'Little Mac' for visibility. ROM uses Nintendo MMC2 mapper (Mapper 9): 8x16KiB PRG-ROM, 16x8KiB CHR-ROM.
